# Baselyne Systems

> ML and data infrastructure for physical AI. We build in weeks what would take your team months — data pipelines, training systems, inference optimization, and fleet deployment. Fixed scope. Defined outcome.

## About

Baselyne Systems is a founder-led infrastructure firm focused on physical AI — robotics, autonomous systems, embodied agents, and world models. We take on hard engineering problems and deliver defined outcomes in fixed timelines.

## Founder

Achyuth Samudrala — Founder & Principal Engineer. Built ML infrastructure at Meta, Isomorphic Labs (Alphabet), and Booking.com — petabyte-scale data platforms, model serving at 100M+ QPS, and ML observability that cut incident detection from days to seconds. Now building infrastructure for physical AI.
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/achyuthsamudrala/

## What We Build

- Sensor data lakes — multi-format ingestion (RLDS, LeRobot, MCAP, HDF5), episode-centric schema, cross-format SQL queries
- Training infrastructure — data lineage, experiment comparison, model registry, reproducibility
- Evaluation & policy gating — automated eval pipelines, regression testing, deployment gates
- Inference optimization — GPU scheduling, model serving, latency profiling
- Edge data management — offline-first sync, priority scoring, resumable upload over intermittent connectivity
- Fleet deployment — model versioning, monitoring, updates without downtime, rollback
- Compliance & audit trail — data lineage from sensor input to model decision, EU AI Act, ISO 42001

## Contact

Schedule a 30-minute call to discuss your physical AI infrastructure challenges.
Calendly: https://calendly.com/achyuthsamudrala/30min
Website: https://baselynesystems.com/contact

## Blog

Technical case studies on infrastructure built by Baselyne.

### Data Infrastructure

#### The Robot Data Lake (April 2026)
Format-agnostic data lake for physical AI — ingesting RLDS, LeRobot, MCAP, and HDF5 into Apache Iceberg tables. 5,121 episodes across 3 formats and 3 robot types queryable in 4-74ms.
URL: https://baselynesystems.com/blog/robot-data-lake

#### Building a High-Throughput CDC Platform (February 2026)
Modular Python CDC platform achieving 38,647 msg/s with config-driven tuning, exactly-once delivery, multi-source support, and Apache Iceberg lakehouse with time travel.
URL: https://baselynesystems.com/blog/cdc-platform

#### Retrieval OS: A Production-Grade Retrieval Runtime (February 2026)
Config versioning, staged rollouts, automatic quality guard-rails, and 2.8ms rollback propagation for RAG systems. 10,785 QPS on a single node with 81.4% natural cache hit rate.
URL: https://baselynesystems.com/blog/retrieval-os

### MLOps & Model Platforms

#### MLOps for Robot Policies (April 2026)
Training pipeline infrastructure for physical AI — data lineage, experiment comparison across policy architectures (MLP 59s/100% vs diffusion 509s/99% vs flow 522s/100% on same versioned data), model registry with stage lifecycle, and full reproducibility records.
URL: https://baselynesystems.com/blog/training-pipeline

#### GateKeeper: Eval-Gated Model Deployment (March 2026)
Eval-gated deployment pipeline that blocks bad models before production. Config-driven quality gates — accuracy, drift, LLM judge, champion vs. challenger. Canary traffic management with auto-rollback.
URL: https://baselynesystems.com/blog/gatekeeper

### AI Infrastructure

#### Edge Sync: Offline-First Data Sync (April 2026)
Offline-first sync daemon with intermittent connectivity. Priority scoring by training value, bounded buffer eviction, resumable chunked upload, two-phase completion. 100% failure data survival while dropping half of all episodes. 9 identified race conditions with specific mitigations.
URL: https://baselynesystems.com/blog/edge-sync

#### Bulkhead: Defense in Depth for Autonomous AI Agents (March 2026)
Four independent enforcement layers for AI agent governance: compiled guardrails evaluated in 184ns, kernel-level iptables egress, append-only audit trail, and non-blocking human-in-the-loop escalation.
URL: https://baselynesystems.com/blog/bulkhead

## Topics We Help With

- Physical AI infrastructure (robotics, autonomous systems, embodied AI, world models)
- Sensor data pipelines and episode storage
- Training infrastructure, data lineage, and reproducibility
- Model evaluation and deployment gating
- Inference optimization and GPU scheduling
- Edge data management and offline-first sync
- Fleet deployment, versioning, and monitoring
- Compliance and audit trails (EU AI Act, ISO 42001)
- Data lake architecture (Apache Iceberg)
- MLOps for physical AI
